Executive Summary

SKRI 0.2650 — WATCH ZONE — THE CANARY IS WATCHING

Sweden's macro environment is in a cautious Watch state in Q3 2026. The composite SKRI of 0.2650 reflects a housing market in cautious recovery (KC_02 = 0.35, HIGHEST WEIGHT 0.30) that remains systemically exposed given Sweden's extreme price-to-income ratios. Riksbank has normalised policy rates after its historic tightening cycle and is watching carefully (KC_01 = 0.30). SEK is stable at USDSEK ~10.5, below the 11.0 stress threshold (KC_03 = 0.25). The Baltic/NATO environment is elevated but calm — Sweden joined NATO in March 2024 and the canary is watching (KC_04 = 0.20). Swedish banking is stable with NPLs well below trigger levels (KC_05 = 0.22).

KC_02 HOUSING: The World's Most Overextended Market

Swedish housing carries the highest weight (0.30) in the SKRI composite — and for critical reasons. Sweden's price-to-income ratio exceeds 400%, placing it among the most overextended housing markets in the entire developed world. Household debt-to-disposable income stands near 180% — a historically extreme level. After prices fell 15-20% from 2022-2023 peaks, there is a cautious recovery underway. But the systemic risk is structural: Swedbank and SEB hold the majority of Sweden's mortgage debt, and any second leg down in housing prices would create direct NPL stress in those banks.

The Riksbank repo rate trajectory is the single most important variable for KC_02: if Riksbank holds too long at elevated rates, the housing recovery stalls and a second leg down becomes probable. Conversely, rate cuts accelerate housing stabilisation — this is the KC_01/KC_02 interdependency that defines the SKRI composite.

SKRI Composite Formula

SKRI = KC_01 × 0.20 + KC_02 × 0.30 + KC_03 × 0.20 + KC_04 × 0.15 + KC_05 × 0.15
     = 0.30 × 0.20 + 0.35 × 0.30 + 0.25 × 0.20 + 0.20 × 0.15 + 0.22 × 0.15
     = 0.0600 + 0.1050 + 0.0500 + 0.0300 + 0.0330
     = 0.2650 WATCH

ZONES: NORMAL < 0.28 · WATCH 0.28-0.55 · ELEVATED 0.55-0.80 · CRITICAL ≥ 0.80
SIGNAL: THE CANARY IS WATCHING

Monetary Policy — Riksbank: The World's Pioneer

The Riksbank (Sveriges Riksbank) was founded in 1668 — making it the world's oldest central bank, predating the Bank of England by 26 years and the US Federal Reserve by 245 years. This historical primacy is not merely ceremonial: Riksbank has repeatedly been the first mover in global monetary policy experiments that the rest of the world followed.

The Three Historic Firsts

1. FIRST CENTRAL BANK (1668) — Founded as Riksens Ständers Bank. The blueprint for all modern central banking institutions.

2. FIRST NEGATIVE INTEREST RATES (2015) — Riksbank cut the repo rate to −0.10% in February 2015, eventually reaching −0.50%. This was unprecedented in 350 years of Riksbank history and was later followed by the ECB, Swiss National Bank, Bank of Japan, and others. The "great monetary experiment" — Sweden ran it first.

3. FIRST TO FULLY EXIT NEGATIVE RATES (2024) — As inflation returned and the tightening cycle concluded, Riksbank was among the first major central banks to fully normalise policy and exit negative rate territory. This positioned Sweden as the leading indicator for the global policy normalisation cycle.

Global implication: When Riksbank pivots, ECB and BOE tend to follow within 6-12 months. Every Riksbank decision is watched as a forward signal by institutional investors worldwide.

Housing Section — KC_02 Deep Dive

KC_01 Riksbank Rate Path / Pivot Weight: 0.20 WATCH Score: 0.30 Contribution: 0.0600

Riksbank is the world's oldest central bank (founded 1668) and the global monetary policy pioneer. It was the FIRST central bank to adopt negative interest rates (2015, at −0.25% then −0.50%) and the FIRST to fully exit negative rates (2024). This makes every Riksbank rate decision a globally-watched leading indicator — Riksbank leads ECB and BOE by 6-12 months historically. The repo rate path determines SEK carry dynamics, housing affordability (KC_02), and bank NIM (KC_05). Current score 0.30 reflects a neutral hold — Riksbank has normalised after the tightening cycle and is watching for inflation consolidation before any further cuts.

TRIGGER THRESHOLDS: Surprise cut >50bps = 0.80 | Cut cycle begins = 0.50 | Hold neutral = 0.30 | Hawkish = 0.20
KC_02 Housing Bubble Deflation★ HIGHEST WEIGHT Weight: 0.30 WATCH Score: 0.35 Contribution: 0.1050

Swedish housing is the DEFINING systemic risk in the SKRI framework — hence the HIGHEST WEIGHT (0.30). Sweden's price-to-income ratio exceeds 400%, making it one of the most overextended housing markets in the entire developed world. Prices fell 15-20% from peak-to-trough in 2022-2023 as Riksbank raised rates aggressively. A second leg down — triggered if Riksbank holds too long or global credit conditions tighten — would create systemic stress across Swedbank and SEB, which together hold the majority of Swedish mortgage debt. Valueguard HOX is the primary real-time tracker. Current score 0.35 reflects a cautious recovery from the 2023 trough — housing is stabilising but vulnerability remains extreme given the debt-to-income ratios (~180% of disposable income).

TRIGGER THRESHOLDS: Prices −20%+ YoY + Swedbank NPL spike = 0.85 | Prices −10-20% = 0.60 | Prices −5-10% = 0.40 | Flat/rising = 0.25
KC_03 SEK FX / Carry Unwind Weight: 0.20 NORMAL Score: 0.25 Contribution: 0.0500

The Swedish krona (SEK) is one of the world's most high-beta G10 currencies — it amplifies global risk-off moves, falling sharply when investor sentiment deteriorates. USDSEK above 11.0 signals elevated stress and often precedes or accompanies Swedish macro deterioration. SEK weakness creates a compounding effect: it depresses Valueguard HOX in USD terms (important for EWD holders), reduces Riksbank's real purchasing power, and increases import costs — which complicates the inflation target management. Current score 0.25 reflects USDSEK near 10.5, in stable territory. A SEK carry unwind (global risk-off + Riksbank easing) would compound KC_02 stress.

TRIGGER THRESHOLDS: USDSEK >11.5 + housing falling = 0.80 | USDSEK >11.0 = 0.50 | USDSEK 10.5-11.0 = 0.30 | Stable = 0.20
KC_04 Baltic Security / NATO Stress Weight: 0.15 NORMAL Score: 0.20 Contribution: 0.0300

Sweden joined NATO in March 2024, becoming the 32nd member. The Baltic Sea is now effectively a NATO lake — but Russian hybrid warfare, undersea cable sabotage, GPS jamming, and cyber operations remain live risks. Gotland island is strategically critical for Baltic sea control and has been reinforced. Notably, KC_04 has an INVERSE BULLISH signal for Saab AB (SAABY): Baltic military escalation directly accelerates Saab's defence contracts — JAS Gripen fighter upgrades, Carl-Gustaf recoilless rifles, NLAW anti-tank weapons, and Archer artillery systems. The EWD/SAABY divergence trade is the clearest signal when KC_04 fires. Current score 0.20 reflects elevated background tensions without a direct incident.

TRIGGER THRESHOLDS: Direct Russian military incident near Sweden = 0.75 | Hybrid warfare confirmed = 0.45 | Tensions elevated = 0.25 | Normal = 0.15
KC_05 Swedish Banking Stress Weight: 0.15 NORMAL Score: 0.22 Contribution: 0.0330

The four Swedish banking pillars — Swedbank, SEB, Handelsbanken, and Nordea (group HQ Stockholm) — together represent one of the most concentrated banking systems in Europe. Swedbank holds the largest Swedish mortgage book and derives 20%+ of revenues from the Baltic states (Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania), creating a KC_04/KC_05 cross-trigger mechanism. SEB is heavily exposed to corporate lending in Sweden and the Nordic region. Handelsbanken uses a highly decentralised model with low risk appetite. Nordea operates as a pan-Nordic bank. Current NPLs are below 2% sector-wide — well within normal range. Score 0.22 reflects residual housing-linked watch: if KC_02 deteriorates sharply, NPLs would lag by 6-12 months before spiking, making this a lagging indicator.

TRIGGER THRESHOLDS: Riksbank systemic risk alert = 0.85 | NPL >4% sector = 0.55 | Individual bank stress = 0.30 | Stable = 0.20

SEK FX / Carry Unwind — KC_03

The Swedish krona (SEK) is a high-beta G10 risk currency — it amplifies global risk sentiment moves by 1.5-2x compared to the euro. When global risk-off episodes occur (credit stress, geopolitical shocks, equity selloffs), SEK weakens disproportionately as investors unwind carry trades and reduce exposure to smaller open economies.

USDSEK above 11.0 is the primary watch threshold for KC_03. Current USDSEK (~10.5) is below this threshold, placing KC_03 in NORMAL territory despite the overall WATCH reading driven by KC_02 housing. A simultaneous housing deterioration and SEK weakness scenario creates a compounding stress pathway: weaker SEK raises import inflation, complicates Riksbank's rate path, and creates dual pressure on Swedish banking NIM and mortgage holders.

Baltic Security / NATO — KC_04 & The Saab Signal

Sweden NATO Accession — March 2024

Sweden became NATO's 32nd member in March 2024, completing the Nordic security bloc (alongside Norway, Denmark, Iceland, and Finland which joined in 2023). The Baltic Sea is now effectively a NATO lake — all littoral states except Russia and Kaliningrad are NATO members.

Strategic implications: Gotland island (positioned at the centre of the Baltic) has been reinforced and provides A2/AD capability. NATO Baltic Air Policing rotates through Sweden. However, Russian hybrid warfare operations — undersea cable sabotage (like the Estonian-Finnish cable cut in late 2024), GPS jamming, and cyber operations — remain live risks that operate below the Article 5 threshold.

★ SAABY INVERSE BULLISH — The Defining SKRI Trade Signal

Saab AB (SAABY on US OTC markets) is the only stock in the 8-stock Sweden impact matrix that is INVERSE BULLISH when KC_04 fires. This means: Baltic military escalation = Saab defence contracts accelerate = SAABY is bullish even as general Swedish equities (EWD) face risk-off selling.

Saab's primary products: JAS 39 Gripen (multirole fighter jet, Sweden and export orders) · Carl-Gustaf M4 (recoilless rifle, widely used by NATO) · NLAW (anti-tank missile system, proven in Ukraine) · Archer (self-propelled howitzer) · GlobalEye (AEW&C airborne surveillance). Each Baltic escalation event accelerates procurement decisions by Sweden, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, and NATO partner nations.

Portfolio Signal: KC_04 ELEVATED = Long SAABY / Short EWD relative value trade. The SAABY/EWD spread is the clearest CIO signal from this canary.

Swedish Banking System — KC_05

Sweden's banking system is highly concentrated around four major institutions:

Swedbank
Largest Swedish mortgage bank by market share. 20%+ revenues from Baltic operations (Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania). KC_04/KC_05 cross-trigger: Baltic escalation = Swedbank Baltic NPL risk. Most exposed to KC_02 housing crash.
SEB
Corporate and investment banking focus. Strong Nordic franchise. Baltic operations significant. Mortgage exposure significant but less concentrated than Swedbank. Stress test: SEB CDS levels are a key watch indicator.
Handelsbanken
Highly decentralised model. Conservative risk appetite. Lowest NPL ratios in the system historically. Swedish mortgage exposure present but managed conservatively. Often seen as the safest Swedish bank.
Nordea (NRDXF)
Group HQ moved to Helsinki 2018 but largest operations remain in Sweden. Pan-Nordic bank. Most directly US-tradeable through NRDXF OTC. Baltic + Swedish housing exposure makes it the primary US transmission vehicle for KC_02 and KC_04/KC_05 stress.

Current sector NPL: <2%. KC_05 trigger: NPL >4% sector-wide. Banking stress is currently classified NORMAL (score 0.22), but housing deterioration would hit NPLs with a 6-12 month lag — making KC_05 the key lagging indicator to watch if KC_02 deteriorates.

★ SAABY INVERSE BULLISH SIGNAL

SAABY is the only stock in this universe where KC_04 Baltic/NATO is +0.80 BULLISH. Baltic escalation = Saab defence contracts accelerate. The EWD/SAABY spread trade — Long SAABY, Short EWD — is the clearest and most unique signal from the Riksbank Sweden Canary. When KC_04 rises above 0.30, activate the divergence trade alert.
